session.name: MPI-64 SESSION 6 — built + committed A1 engine-drop recovery (0c243f5); ran a full live fresh-volume validation of the MPI-75 v0.4.0 image; consolidated all open items into OPEN-ITEMS.md; handed remote-uninstall (L6) to the MPI-75 agent; promoted slide-over fix to MPI-79.

goal.original: Continue MPI-64 RunPod remote engine. This session: consolidated the scattered open items into a durable register, coded A1 (engine-drop recovery), and ran ONE expensive fresh-volume live cycle to validate everything that needed a live Pod (MPI-75 v0.4.0 image + A1 + G5 + F8 + remote T2V).
goal.summary: A1 is DONE + LIVE-VERIFIED + COMMITTED (0c243f5). The fresh-volume live session passed A1, B-T2V, aria2c(G4), volume-delete(G5), delete-on-quit(F8); FOUND remote-uninstall broken (L6/G3, handed to MPI-75 agent) + Blackwell create-400 (L1) + 4 minor (L2-L5). RunPod is now the shared v1.1.0 trunk. Two agents finishing concurrently: MPI-75 (image/uninstall coupling) and MPI-79 (slide-container). Next session resumes the MPI-64 masterplan AFTER those agents finish.

Drop -> sticky remote·disconnected hero + IDLE·Disconnected status + ui:warning toast + clean gen-end, no false local, no relaunch; manual reconnect -> online + model panel re-hydrate across a GPU switch.

next_action.description: Resume the MPI-64 masterplan from OPEN-ITEMS.md AFTER the MPI-75 and MPI-79 agents have finished (check git log on RunPod for their commits + read any new open_messages). FIRST re-read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/index.json fresh + tasks/MPI-64/OPEN-ITEMS.md (the consolidated register) + current-architecture.md §10 header. Then pick the next item: strongest candidates are B1 (remote input-asset transfer — unblocks remote I2V, the big remaining Phase-4 feature) or L1/L2 (Blackwell create-400 + surface RunPod's error body). Confirm L6 (remote uninstall) status with the MPI-75 agent before re-testing it. Brief before code (mpi-continue gate). GIT: RunPod is the shared v1.1.0 trunk — stage by EXPLICIT PATH only, commit your own files, --no-verify per convention; never git add -A.

key_decisions: ONE expensive fresh-volume live cycle was used to validate everything needing a live Pod at once (MPI-75 v0.4.0 + A1 + G5 + F8 + T2V) rather than repeating the costly volume-delete/reinstall.

A1 reconnect is MANUAL (Settings -> Connect), not auto — never surprise-rebill a Pod the user may have wanted stopped. The drop is a sticky 'disconnected' phase reusing the existing MPI-73 phase machinery; A1 deliberately does NOT reimplement the connect orchestration (Settings owns it with all its billing/availability guards).
A1 fix is renderer-only and reuses comfyController's already-emitted remote:engine-dropped (previously no subscriber) — minimal new surface.

L6 (remote uninstall) is MPI-75's app-side coupling, not MPI-64's — the stale 'endpoint doesn't exist' fallback predates the v0.4.0 /wrapper/models/delete ship. Handed off, not fixed here, to avoid colliding with the MPI-75 agent's backend files.
RAM is the OOM wall for video, re-confirmed live (T2V peaked 92% RAM / 14% VRAM; ~35GiB cache held after a completed gen = D1 stacking). Advise 64GB+ container-RAM Pods for video.
RunPod is the shared v1.1.0 integration trunk; master is patch-only with merge-back. Concurrent agents share the working tree -> stage by explicit path.
constraints: RunPod branch is SHARED by concurrent agents (MPI-64, MPI-75, MPI-79). STAGE BY EXPLICIT PATH, commit only your own files, git commit --no-verify; NEVER git add -A. Others' uncommitted work (e.g. MPI-75's routes/remoteProxy.js) sits in the same tree.
NEVER run autonomous Pod create/delete/stop or image builds — the USER runs ALL live Pod/volume/billing ops and image builds; Claude drives steps + observes.
Backend changes (routes/, server.js, main.js) need a FULL app restart; renderer changes (js/, css/) reload with Ctrl+R. The MPI-75 WRAPPER_VERSION bump in routes/remoteProxy.js is a backend change.
Backend logger (routes/logger.js) + clientLogger warn/info take only (category, message). PowerShell-via-Bash is DENIED in this env (use the Read tool for app.log, not Get-Content).
Commit only when the USER asks (A1 commit was user-authorized this session).

Current live volume: cubric-vision-EU-RO-1 (id 14kabihaki), 80GB, EU-RO-1, holds the Wan 2.2 T2V model (uninstall didn't delete it). Real volume path on the Pod = /workspace/mpi_models (UNDERSCORE); models under diffusion_models/ + vae/ + text_encoders/; manifest at /workspace/cubric/manifest.json.
B1 unwritten -> remote I2V gen is NOT testable; only T2V is. Image gen already works remotely.

resume_prompt: You are resuming MPI-64 (RunPod Remote Engine) on branch RunPod (the SHARED v1.1.0 integration trunk) in the Cubric-Vision Electron app (c:/AI/Mpi/Cubric-Vision). Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/f36947a0-283b-4d6e-ad7a-c3b0d84a8fd6.json, then RE-READ .agents/mpi-kanban/state/index.json fresh + tasks/MPI-64/OPEN-ITEMS.md (the consolidated open-items register — READ IT FIRST, it is the single source of truth) + current-architecture.md §10 SESSION 6, then use mpi-continue. STATUS: SESSION 6 shipped + live-verified + COMMITTED (0c243f5) A1 engine-drop recovery — an out-of-band remote WS drop (container OOM) now shows a sticky 'remote · disconnected' hero + 'IDLE · Disconnected' status + a ui:warning toast instead of a false 'local · offline', with manual reconnect (Settings → Connect) that re-hydrates the model panel; renderer-only (js/shell.js _initEngineDropRecovery, heroStats.js, statusBar.js). A full live fresh-volume cycle on the new MPI-75 v0.4.0 image (wrapper 0.2.3) PASSED: remote T2V gen (RAM peaked 92%=53.68/57.74GiB, no OOM — RAM is the wall), aria2c fast download (G4), volume-delete (G5), delete-on-quit (F8); cu124/cu128 arch routing + Step 4.3 self-heal + D1 cache-stacking all confirmed live. FOUND + HANDED OFF: remote model UNINSTALL not working (L6/G3) — handed to the MPI-75 agent via message 9e2964f7 (stale 'endpoint doesn't exist' fallback in downloadManager.js:962-967 / remoteModels.js:272-275 now that v0.4.0 ships /wrapper/models/delete, plus a 2-step-confirm re-test). Also found: Blackwell RTX PRO 4500 create-400 (L1, Blackwell-specific — L4+2000Ada create clean), error-body dropped from log/UI (L2), + L3-L5 minor. H3 slide-over-closes promoted to its own card MPI-79 (a parallel session is doing it; brief at tasks/MPI-79/brief.md). DO NOT resume until the MPI-75 and MPI-79 agents have finished — check git log on RunPod + new open_messages first. Then pick the next masterplan item from OPEN-ITEMS.md: strongest candidates are B1 (remote input-asset transfer — unblocks remote I2V, the big remaining Phase-4 feature) or L1/L2 (Blackwell-400 + surface RunPod's create-error body). GOTCHAS: RunPod is shared by concurrent agents → stage by EXPLICIT PATH, commit only your own files, --no-verify, NEVER git add -A; the USER runs ALL live Pod/volume/image ops; backend (routes/) changes need a FULL app restart, renderer reloads with Ctrl+R; logger warn/info = (category, message) only; PowerShell-via-Bash is DENIED (use the Read tool for app.log); commit only when the USER asks. Brief before code (mpi-continue gate).
